summary: DeVault advocates for the 'contrib' directory convention in open source projects, a lightly-organized space for community-contributed scripts, configs, and tools that don't belong in the main codebase. These contributions receive minimal code review, no maintenance guarantees, and aren't wired into build systems or documentation. The pattern lets useful but niche tools live alongside the project without imposing maintenance burden on core maintainers. He illustrates the concept with examples from Sway's contrib folder, showing how it accommodates scripts in different languages, with varying quality levels, and for diverse use cases.
key_points: The contrib directory is an established but rarely discussed convention for housing community contributions that don't fit as official project features
Items in contrib should not be part of the build system, automated testing, documentation, or packages — they carry no maintenance guarantee
Contrib fills the gap between 'too small for its own project' and 'not appropriate for upstream' — a place to drop useful scripts that save others time
The maintenance burden for contrib entries lies entirely with the users, not the project maintainers
If a contribution warrants greater maintenance, it should become a standalone project with its own issue tracking and releases
money_quotes: There is no guarantee whatsoever of workitude or maintenance for anything found in contrib.
The idea is to provide a place to drop a couple of files which might save a future someone facing similar problems from doing all of the work themselves.
If the contributor wants to take on a greater maintenance burden, this kind of stuff is better suited to a standalone project, with its own issue tracking, releases, and so on.
Things in contrib should not be wired into your build system, shouldn't be part of your automated testing, shouldn't be included in your documentation, and should not be installed with your packages.
If you just wrote a little script and want somewhere to drop it so that others may find it useful, then contrib is the place for you.
Adding this upstream would introduce a lot of third-party dependencies for a minor convenience.
key_insight: Open source projects benefit from a low-ceremony 'contrib' directory that gives community scripts a home without imposing maintenance obligations on core maintainers.
